title: Two Sum
blurb: Given an array of numbers and a target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target. Yes, this again. You will see this in your dreams.

title: Add Two Numbers
blurb: Two numbers are stored as backwards linked lists because someone hates you. Add them and return a backwards linked list.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
blurb: Find the longest run of characters with no repeats. Sliding window. Everyone says 'just use a sliding window' as if that means anything to a scared human.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
blurb: Find the median of two sorted arrays in logarithmic time. The 'Hard' tag is the only honest thing on this website.

title: Valid Parentheses
blurb: Given a string of brackets, decide if they are balanced. Push, pop, despair. A stack, allegedly.

title: Merge k Sorted Lists
blurb: Merge k sorted linked lists into one. A heap is involved. Your will to live is not.

title: Trapping Rain Water
blurb: Given elevation heights, compute how much rainwater is trapped. The metaphor writes itself.

title: Climbing Stairs
blurb: You can climb 1 or 2 stairs at a time. How many ways to the top? It's Fibonacci. It's always Fibonacci. They lied and called it DP.

title: Word Break
blurb: Decide if a string can be segmented into dictionary words. DP over a string, which is a fancy way of saying suffering with a cache.

title: LRU Cache
blurb: Implement a cache that evicts the least recently used item. Hashmap plus doubly linked list, because a normal cache would be too kind.

title: Number of Islands
blurb: Count islands in a grid of land and water. Flood fill until you, too, feel like an isolated landmass surrounded by nothing.

title: Course Schedule
blurb: Given course prerequisites, decide if you can finish them all. Topological sort. Detect the cycle. The cycle is your career.

title: Coin Change
blurb: Fewest coins to make an amount. Unbounded knapsack cosplaying as a friendly money problem.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
blurb: Turn a tree into a string and back. A problem invented purely to see you cry in a 45-minute window.

title: Reverse Integer
blurb: Reverse the digits of a 32-bit integer and handle overflow. Rated Medium so you feel bad when it wrecks you on edge cases.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
blurb: Given daily prices, maximize one buy-low-sell-high profit. If you could actually do this you would not be grinding LeetCode.

title: Container With Most Water
blurb: Two pointers find the biggest area between vertical lines. The container holds water; you hold back tears.

title: 3Sum
blurb: Two Sum's older sibling who peaked in an interview in 2017 and won't stop talking about it. Find triplets summing to zero.

title: Group Anagrams
blurb: Group words that are anagrams of each other. Sort each word as a key. Congratulations, you invented a hashmap.

title: Maximum Subarray
blurb: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum. Kadane's algorithm, named after a man who has caused more interview anxiety than any war.

title: Product of Array Except Self
blurb: Return an array where each element is the product of all others, and no you may not use division, because we must suffer.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
blurb: Find the kth largest element. Just sort it. No, use a heap. No, quickselect. No, cry. The correct answer is cry.

title: Word Ladder
blurb: Transform one word into another one letter at a time via a dictionary. BFS over words, because Scrabble was not stressful enough.

title: Edit Distance
blurb: Minimum edits to turn one string into another. A 2D DP table and the crushing knowledge that your resume needs the same treatment.

title: Sliding Window Maximum
blurb: Max of every window of size k. A monotonic deque is involved. So is the slow realization you should have studied biology.

title: Regular Expression Matching
blurb: Implement regex matching with '.' and '*'. The problem that has ended more interviews, friendships, and Saturdays than any other.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
blurb: Find the longest palindrome inside a string. Expand around center, or look up Manacher's algorithm and immediately forget it.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
blurb: Find the max path sum in a tree, where a path can start and end anywhere. The path leads directly to a cover letter.

title: Spiral Matrix
blurb: Return the matrix elements in spiral order. Four pointers, off-by-one errors, and the distinct feeling you are the one going in circles.

title: Rotate Image
blurb: Rotate an n x n matrix 90 degrees in place. Transpose, then reverse. Your career trajectory: transpose, then reverse.
